title: iPhone 17 Pro/Max Battery Saving Tips: Boost Now!
transcript: In this video, I'm going to show you how to improve your iPhone's battery life. So, let's get started. First, force restart your iPhone. Quickly, press and release the volume up button. Then, press and release the volume down button. After that, press and hold the side button until you see the Apple logo. Let go once the logo appears and wait for your iPhone to finishing. Now unlock your iPhone and start using it again. Second, turn off background app refresh. Launch the settings app on your iPhone. Scroll down and tap general. Then scroll to background app refresh and manually turn off the toggle next to the apps you don't use every day, like shopping apps or games. Whenever you install a new app, background app refresh usually turns on automatically. To stop all apps from refreshing in the background, tap background app refresh again and select off. The third tip is to change your location services settings. Launch the settings app. Scroll down and tap privacy and security. Then tap location services. Check each app and set it to while using the app. only allow always for apps that truly need to run in the background like navigation, fitness trackers, or safety apps. The fourth fix is to adjust your display settings. Launch the settings app. Scroll to display and brightness. Then scroll down and tap autolock. Choose the minimum time so your iPhone locks automatically and saves battery. Now go back and turn off raise tow. You can also lower your brightness manually or turn on auto brightness. Remember, using high brightness all the time drains your battery faster. Keeping it at a moderate level really helps. Next, check your daily battery usage. Launch the settings app. Scroll down and tap battery. Review the daily usage chart to see which apps are using the most power. This helps you figure out what's draining your battery and make smarter usage choices. Also, disable app notifications for unnecessary apps. Launch the settings app. Scroll down to notifications. Again, scroll down and find the notification style. We can disable notifications for individual apps. Just tap the app name and turn off the toggle next to allow notifications. Tip number eight is to set fetch new mail manually. Launch the settings app on your iPhone. Scroll down to the bottom and tap on the app section. Afterward, scroll down and tap on mail. Then select mail accounts and tap on fetch new data. Scroll down and select the manually option for fetching new data. You can also choose a particular mail account and select the manual option. The ninth tip is to check and update to the latest iOS version. Launch the settings app, tap general, and then software update. If an update is available, tap download and install. If your iPhone's already running the latest version, skip this step. Sometimes after a big update, your battery might drain a little faster for a few days. That's normal. Just give it some time to settle. If the battery still drains too quickly, try using adaptive power. This feature automatically adjusts performance and activates low power mode when your battery reaches 20%. If none of these tips work, it's time to contact Apple support. Open Safari and go to the Apple support website. Sign in with your Apple ID, select your device, and describe your issue. You can read support articles, start a chat, or schedule a call. If that doesn't fix it, book a Genius Bar appointment at your nearest Apple store to get it checked or repaired. Thanks for watching. Don't forget to like and subscribe for more iPhone tips and tricks.

title: How to Replace iPhone 17 Pro Screen | Step-by-Step Tutorial
transcript: Hello everyone, and welcome to HowFixit. If  your iPhone 17 Pro has a cracked, broken,   or defective screen, don’t worry — I’m here to  help. In this video, I’ll walk you through a   step-by-step screen replacement so you can restore  your iPhone to full working order yourself.  Before we get started, here’s  what you’ll need for this repair:  A precision screwdriver set, plastic pry  tools, a pair of tweezers, a suction cup, and   a screen disassembly tool — essential for safely  separating the display from the frame. To restore   water resistance, you’ll need adhesive sealing  tape. I’ve included links to all the tools and   replacement parts in the video description below. For the best results, I highly recommend using   a magnetic mat to organize screws and  small components during disassembly.   The iPhone 17 Pro contains dozens of screws  of varying sizes, and a magnetic mat helps   keep everything secure and properly organized  for reassembly. Alright, let’s get started.  First, power off your iPhone and remove the  two screws located next to the USB-C port.  Next, heat the screen. Warming the  lower edge softens the waterproof   adhesive — apply gentle, even heat for  60 to 90 seconds at about 90 °C (194 °F).  Place a suction cup near the bottom edge of  the screen, then insert a thin blade into the   gap between the chassis and the display to  loosen the adhesive. Once a small gap forms,   switch to a plastic pry tool. Keep  the surface warm as you work your   way around the edges — a well-heated  screen separates much more easily.  The screen is tightly glued to the frame,  so apply steady, even pressure as you go.  Ensure the surface stays warm  as you work around the edges;   this helps make adhesive removal easier.  At the top edge, two hinges  connect the display to the housing;   once you reach them, push  slightly downward to release.  Lift the display like a book from the side.  Remove the screw securing the bracket over   the display and front-sensor connectors,  then carefully disconnect the cables.  Now, let’s transfer the proximity and ambient  light sensor flex cable to the new screen.  Remove the two screws, then slightly heat the flex  cable with a hair dryer to loosen the adhesive.  Carefully detach the front sensor  and flex cable from the old screen.  Ensure the surface stays warm as you work; this  helps make detaching the flex cable easier.  Now, you can proceed to install the new  screen. I've included a link to a compatible   replacement screen for the iPhone 17  Pro in the video description below.  Attach the front sensor to the new screen.  Gently warm the flex cable to soften  the adhesive for better bonding.  Secure the cable with the appropriate screws.  Reconnect the display cables to the  logic board and close the iPhone.  Power on the device and test the new screen. After the replacement, you’ll need to calibrate   the new screen using Repair Assistant. Go to Settings, then select General,   and then the About section. You will see a new section — Parts & Service   History. Go to this section, follow  the calibration prompts, then test the   touchscreen for dead zones or unresponsive areas  to confirm everything is functioning properly.  Once testing is complete,  let’s restore water resistance.  Remove the old adhesive tape from the  iPhone’s frame. Once the iPhone is opened,   the adhesive tape loses its sealing properties  and can no longer protect your device from   water and dust. To restore water resistance,  you’ll need to apply new waterproof sealing   tape around the frame. However, if you  don’t have the new adhesive tape on hand,   it’s best to leave the old tape in  place until you’re ready to replace it.  Use a cotton swab dipped in isopropyl alcohol   (91% or higher) to clean any  remaining residue from the frame.  Place the cooling plate with the battery into  the iPhone and secure it with fourteen screws.  Carefully align the new adhesive tape with the  edges of the frame, ensuring proper placement.  Press down along the perimeter using  a plastic spudger to secure it firmly.  Once the tape is applied, peel off  the top liner to expose the adhesive.  Reconnect the display and sensor connectors,  replace the bracket, and tighten the screw.  Press gently along the screen edges to ensure a  snug seal, then reinstall the two screws near the   charging port to finish assembly. If you found this guide helpful,   give us a thumbs up and subscribe for  more tech tips and guides. Visit our   website for more insights and Do-It-Yourself  guides. Remember — every tech challenge is an   opportunity to learn and grow. Thanks for watching  HowFixit, and we’ll see you in the next one!

title: iPhone 17 Pro – You NEED to Know This Before Buying!
transcript: The iPhone 17 Pro is exactly 7 grams heavier than the 16 Pro. So, you will notice a few grams heavier. So, over the past 2 weeks, the only thing I've really noticed is that the phone does feel a little bit different in the hand. When you take it out of the case, it does have a slightly different feel to it and the corners a little bit more cornered off, so to speak. So, the phone feels a little bit smoother than the 16 Pro, which means that it does feel a little bit more kind of, in my opinion, premium and a little softer for day-to-day usage. I don't think you'll really have an issue with the weight or the actual form factor of the design. The one thing I will say is that the plateau on the back of the phone does kind of not interfere with my experience, but I am someone who kind of holds my phone in a specific way where my pinky and my back of my hand kind of inter collide with the plateau. And this hasn't really done anything with it. It actually sometimes makes it more stable in my hand, but it's just something I've had to get over because the past iPhones have never had a plateau where the camera X actually expands over the entire back of the phone. In terms of the display, so we do have a 3000 nits brightness display on this current model that is a,000 nits higher than before. So over the past 2 weeks, the phone has been a little bit easier to see in broad daylight. However, I wouldn't say this is something that is going to be a total gamecher for you. It's more of something that might be kind of a nice surprise rather than something that's a complete substantial difference. So, at the end of the day, the display for me, there hasn't been a ton of changes besides the brightness and the anti-reflective. So, we're not looking at something that's completely new to really kind of analyze that further. The A19 Pro chip for me personally has been a subtle um and kind of unsubstantial difference from the A chip on the 16 Pro. For me, day-to-day usage has been just a little bit more snappy. Games are definitely easy to play and I would say overall the phone just feels a little bit more intuitive, snappy, and just everything is just a touch away, which is really not something that's I think has been significant. The only thing I will say that has been kind of noticeable is basically multitasking seems a little bit smoother on the 17 Pro and that's probably due that 12 GB of RAM increase. However, I would not say that you're going to notice a huge difference unless you are coming from something like a 15 or 14 Pro. So, as far as the cameras go, the 17 Pro has a new camera system [music] featuring three 48 megapixel cameras, which is now touting an 8 times optical quality lens. The internet and Apple, and I looked it up a little bit, the research is stating that the new 8x optical lens isn't actually an optical lens of 8x. title: iPhone 17/17 Pro/Pro Max: How to Open Camera Settings
transcript: Hey, what's up guys? So on this video we will see how to open the camera settings on the iPhone 17, 17 Pro and the 17 Pro Max. For that just open settings and in the settings you will find the option of camera. Just tap on it and from here you will find all the camera settings on your iPhone. For an example you can change the resolution of the video. You can change the resolution of the slow motion videos. You can select the resolution for the cinematic mode. Also, you can change the format of the photos and much more. And this is how you can open the camera settings on the iPhone 17, 17 Pro, and the 17 Pro Max. Make sure to like, comment, and subscribe. Thank you for watching.

title: Oppo Find X9 Pro 比較レビュー)もはやiPhone 17 Pro Maxと一緒に使うのが最強なのかも
transcript: Hello, this is Gajikichi.  It's likely that they waited and watched last year , but they have released the OP FindX8, their first flagship model in the domestic market in about four years, and the customer survey included questions about expanding their lineup, so depending on sales, they may strengthen their lineup in the future, including adding foldable models .  This time, I would like to do a comparative review of the OPPO Find X9 Pro and the iPhone 17 Pro Max .  Thank you very much. [Music] Looking at recent Chinese products, they have been strengthening their compatibility with Apple products , so even if you replace your smartphone you can still use the Apple ecosystem , and it seems like they are recommending having two phones, an iPhone and a smartphone .  First, let's look at the design, but the difference in case size is even greater than the specs .  Apple has adopted an aluminum unibody design as one of its heat countermeasures, which means that the thickness of the phone has increased significantly compared to the previous model, perhaps to expose the aluminum or to secure more internal space .  As a result, it is quite thick among flagship models and I think it will be a choice for certain users.  I'm often told that I have small hands for a man, so operating the iPhone 17 Pro Max with one hand is surprisingly difficult.  It might be different if you could swipe from right to left to perform the back gesture, but since you can't, it feels even more awkward to handle, and the OPO Find X9 Pro feels overwhelmingly easier to hold .  On the other hand, the side frames of the iPhone 17 Pro Max are rounded, which makes them fit easily.  Regarding the physical buttons, the OPO Find X9 Pro has a design that is very much like the iPhone, with the camera controls on the screen, but the position of the volume buttons seems more like an Android .  As for the back of the device, the iPhone 17 Pro Max has a unibody design, so only a portion of it is made of glass .  I guess this was probably unavoidable in order to accommodate YS charging, but it's a matter of personal preference.  I'm not sure if this is technically possible , but I think the impression would be different if it were made entirely of aluminum. Another design difference is that the OPO Find X9 Pro has a square shape .  According to O, the design was made possible by miniaturizing the camera components, but it is believed that the change was made to make the internal space more efficient due to the inclusion of a large-capacity battery .  It may just be a coincidence , but the OPPO Find X9 Pro has a design more similar to previous iPhones .  By the way, when measuring the thickness of the body using the camera bump , the iPhone 17 Pro Max is 14.0mm, while the OPO Find X9 Pro is 12.7mm, making it thinner despite having a larger sensor and a larger battery.  In the end, the camera base of the iPhone 17 Pro Max has become larger, but the camera lens still protrudes .  As a result, it still wobbles when placed on a table, just like the previous model, and is not very stable.  By the way, in terms of durability, the OPPO Find X9 Pro is IP compatible, so you can take underwater photos.   On the other hand, the back panel is made of glass, but it is unclear what kind of glass is used in the specifications .  Above all, the OPPO Find X9 Pro has a more luxurious design and is easier to handle.  Next, regarding the display, if we refer to overseas websites, the aspect ratio of the OPPO Find X9 Pro is 19.61:9, while that of the iPhone 17 Pro Max is 19.5: 9, so there is almost no difference in the specs .  Again, when comparing the bezels on the flat display, the OPFinD X9 Pro is slightly slimmer, but the display size is 0.2 inches different.  By the way, the Oppo Find X9 Pro uses Gorilla Glass Vita II while the iPhone 17 Pro Max uses Ceramic Shield II, but it is unclear how much difference there actually is in durability. The iPhone 17 Pro Max also has an anti-reflective coating, and although using a protective film is honestly pointless, the fact that it does not have an in-screen fingerprint sensor means that you can choose the one you like.  Then, to check the content display, I played a YouTube video and the colors looked almost the same.  In fact, even though it is set to automatic adjustment, the difference in display brightness is what really bothers me. By the way, if you refer to the test results on an overseas website, the iPhone 17 Pro Max has an automatic battery life of 1,000 days, while the OPPO Find X9 Pro has a slightly longer life of 1,175 days .  According to an overseas website, the iPhone 17 Pro Max may have further optimized maneuverability by adopting an anti-reflective coating , making it easier to see in darker conditions than before because it is less reflective .  Also, even though the brightness is as specified, it is rated as lasting for a fairly short time, which is not very generous.  However, when comparing the two indoors, the iPhone 17 Pro Max often feels brighter .  Additionally, even outdoors, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is often brighter than the OPPO Find X9 Pro, but in my environment where I mostly use it indoors, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is brighter and easier to see .  Both have excellent video capabilities, and the iPhone 17 Pro Max supports HDR even when not in full screen, so the iPhone is easier to see the size of fish for people who enjoy videos without using a heavy camera.   On the other hand, although I can't personally experience this, the OPO FindX9Pro supports 2160Hz PWM long-distance lighting, so it's a good choice for people with sensitive eyes, and it also has an interesting feature that helps prevent motion sickness when riding in a car. As for the basic specifications, details are unknown as Apple has not announced the support period .  However, considering EU regulations, it will support updates every 5 years at least, which is the same as the OPOFX9Pro.  By the way, iOS does not separate major and security updates like Android does .  And a major drawback of the OPPO FindX9Pro is that it is unclear whether there is a Japanese version .  This is just speculation, as there was no movement from OPJapan at the time the video was filmed, but I think there may be a time lag between the OPFU X9 and the global model as they try to make it compatible with FeliCa.  So, if they are spending money on the domestic release of the OPFind X9, it seems unlikely that there will be an OPFind X9 Pro .  Aside from those of me who are experts in using overseas smartphones, for many users, if it is not released domestically it will not be possible to compete on the same level, so I would like it to be released even without FeliCa . And when it comes to AI, I get the impression that it is lagging behind Samsung and Google .  However, compared to Apple , I think it gives the impression of being much more comprehensive.  The circle search feature on Android is difficult to use with Apple's restrictions because it requires the camera to be activated .  At the very least, I think the gap between iOS and Android AI will widen the further Google continues its development .  A major strength of C-OS 16 is its enhanced compatibility with Apple products When I activated my iPhone 17 Pro Max, I lost my SIM card, so now I'm using it as a secondary phone, and being able to share data with Apple products is incredibly convenient.  Also, because I also use a Mac and iPad, there is a synergistic effect and it's great that I can get along well with Apple products. In terms of capacity, the iPhone 17 Pro Max available in Japan is overwhelmingly more powerful.  However, I think the iPhone 17 Pro Max's maximum 2 TB model is a bit excessive.  On the other hand, the global version of the OPPO Find X9 Pro is a 512GB model and only comes in two colors, which is a bit disappointing .  Regardless of whether I'll buy it or not, it's a shame that the OPFinD X9 Pro's hero color, red, is n't available. To check the level of heat generation, we ran the benchmark three times in a row to apply load.  Incidentally, it is said that the measurement methods are different, and simply comparing the scores is almost meaningless .  When the external temperature was measured after each measurement , the OPPO Findx 9 Pro was 40.4°, 44.14°, and 44.1°.   On the other hand, the iPhone 17 Pro Max had a large difference in how much heat it felt, with temperatures ranging from 33.03°C, 36.5°C, and 37.2°C.  We then put it through the 3D Mark World Life Stress Test to see how long it would last .  Whether it's the score or the stability rate, the OPO FindX9Pro is only slightly higher, so there is almost no difference.  By the way, when we measured the temperature after the measurement was completed, the OPPO Find X9 Pro was 38.2°C and the iPhone 17 Pro Max was 33.6°C, which is an overwhelming difference when placed under load in this benchmark test .  It may be different when playing games for a long time, but the iPhone 17 Pro Max is more stable.  I also tried Geekbench 6, which is said to use the same measurement method, but there was a slight difference in the scores.  The OPO FindX9Pro is in balanced mode , but it falls just short .  Next, looking at the battery, the OPPO FindX9 Pro has an overwhelmingly larger battery capacity .  In addition to the difference in battery capacity , there is also a big difference in battery life, with the iPhone 17 Pro Max lasting hours and 58 minutes in a battery test conducted on an external website, while the OPO Find X9 Pro lasted 21 hours and 57 minutes.  I have n't been able to use the OPPO FindX9Pro at all, so I'll have to wait and see, but there's an overwhelming difference .  However, this is not so much that the iPhone 17 Pro Max is bad, but rather that the OPPO Find X9 Pro is just too good. By the way, I do n't think there's much difference between the iPhone 17 Pro Max and the one I use .  On the other hand, if you plan to use it for a long time, the quality of the battery is something you should be concerned about.  Silicon carbon batteries are said to deteriorate quickly , and while the OPPO FX9 Pro has revealed that it maintains 80% of its capacity even after 1,300 charging cycles , it is unclear how this will affect the iPhone 17 Pro Max. However, EU regulations also require that the battery last at least 80% after 800 charging cycles, so I think it's even better than that.  To be honest, if the charging cycle doesn't change much, Oho is better due to the difference in capacity. As for charging speed, the iPhone 17 Pro Max reaches 65% capacity in 30 minutes and takes 72 minutes to fully charge.  On the other hand, the OPO FindX9Pro takes 67 minutes to fully charge, while it only takes 30 minutes to reach 54% charge .  It's amazing that the OPO FindX9Pro is faster despite the difference in battery capacity of nearly 2400mA .  Regarding wireless charging, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is G2 compatible and can charge at a maximum of 25W, while it is unclear whether the OPPO Find X9 Pro is G2 certified, but its unique design allows it to charge at a maximum of 50W, which is a major advantage.  Also, the OPO FindX9Pro's camera design makes it easy to use as it is magnetically compatible.   For biometric authentication, the OPFindX9Pro supports an ultrasonic on-screen fingerprint sensor and 2D fingerprint authentication.  On the other hand, it can be said that fingerprint authentication is more convenient , but the advantage of the iPhone 17 Pro Max is that all authentication can be completed with Face ID, and Android's 2D facial recognition is only for basic unlocking.  We also performed a speaker test with the volume set to 70%, so please check it out. The Oppo Find X9 Pro also supports Telemarque, so I personally find it very versatile. It's simply difficult to press when holding the device horizontally, so I wish the OPO in particular had made it a point to use it as a camera .
